seoTitle: Surf Beach vs Surry Hills — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Surf Beach and Surry Hills. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Surf Beach vs Surry Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Surf Beach and Surry Hills.
heading: Surf Beach vs Surry Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Surf Beach, New South Wales and Surry Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Surry Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Surf Beach (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Surf Beach or Surry Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Surf Beach has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Surry Hills scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Surf Beach scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Surry Hills scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Surf Beach or Surry Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Surry Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
